title: Add hyperparameter profile for 2080 Ti (11GB VRAM)
completion_summary: trainer/profiles/profile_2080ti.yaml: batch_size 2 + grad_accum 4 (effective batch 8), load_in_4bit true, lora_rank 16, max_seq_len 2048, lr 2e-4, warmup 5, max_steps 60, seed 3407. Loader integration: train.py grew --profile <path> + --load-in-4bit/--no-load-in-4bit flags. Two-pass argparse: load_profile() reads YAML, _apply_profile() calls parser.set_defaults(**profile) so the profile layers on top of built-ins but CLI flags still win. Unknown profile keys are reported as a WARNING (not fatal) so profiles can carry comments/metadata. The OOM-on-11GB acceptance criterion isn't testable on this CPU-only host -- run on the 2080 Ti when one's available. Three new pytest tests cover profile precedence (CLI > profile > default), unknown-key tolerance, and that every shipped YAML loads + has lora_rank=16. Deliverables: trainer/profiles/profile_2080ti.yaml + the --profile loader wired into train.py.
acceptance_criteria: Batch size 2-4, gradient accumulation 2-4, QLoRA only
Training completes without OOM on 11GB 2080 Ti
Profile is configurable via CLI or config file
